premise: Direct soft-mode eigenvector regression is ill-posed (72% of materials have degenerate softest optical modes). Try the user's cascade: simple classifier screen -> fine regression.

tier1_instability_classifier.task: predict whether a structure has a Gamma soft mode (softest optical freq < -0.1 THz) = latent ferroelectric / dynamical instability
tier1_instability_classifier.verdict: WORKS - useful screening (top decile ~43% hit rate vs 9% base rate)

tier2_participation_tensor_regression.task: degeneracy-invariant per-atom soft-mode participation tensor S_i = sum_(soft) e_i (x) e_i (rank-2, equivariant)
tier2_participation_tensor_regression.verdict: FAILS - stays below the mean-tensor baseline; fine mode shape not learnable from 1534 diverse materials

conclusion: Consistent across ALL experiments: COARSE/scalar/classification tasks are learnable (instability presence AUC 0.77; band gap R2 0.6-0.7; Born charge Z* R2 0.95; switchability AUC 0.8), while FINE geometric/directional tasks are NOT (Ps vector, soft-mode eigenvector, participation tensor) with ~200-1500 diverse materials. The cascade Tier-1 screen is the usable discovery tool; Tier-2 fine regression needs active-learning data growth (DFT loop) or per-chemistry/per-prototype specialization.
usable_deliverables: Equivariant latent-instability screening classifier (Tier-1): screen non-polar crystals for hidden soft modes (AUC 0.77, 4.8x enrichment)
Equivariant Born-charge -> quantum-free spontaneous polarization (Z* R2 0.95, 10k materials)
